2605.01256.title: GIFT: Guided Fine-Tuning and Transfer for Enhancing Instruction-Tuned Language Models
2605.01256.paper_summary: A promising paradigm for adapting instruction-tuned language models is to learn task-specific updates on a pretrained base model and subsequently merge them into the instruction-tuned model. However, existing approaches typically treat the instruction-tuned model as a passive target that is only involved at the final merging stage, without guiding the training process. We propose GIFT (Guided Fine-Tuning and Transfer), a simple and efficient framework that incorporates guidance from the instruction model into task adaptation. GIFT fine-tunes a low-rank adapter on the pretrained base model using confidence signals derived from the instruction-tuned model. The learned adapter is then merged into the instruction-tuned model, yielding task-specialized models that preserve general instruction-following behavior. We evaluate GIFT on mathematical and knowledge-intensive benchmarks across multiple model families and scales. Results show that GIFT consistently outperforms direct fine-tuning and representative transfer-based baselines, while maintaining robust generalization and favorable test-time scaling behavior.
2605.01256.paper_summary_zh: 一個有前景的範式是對指令調整的語言模型進行適應，具體方法是在預訓練的基礎模型上學習任務特定的更新，然後將其合併到指令調整模型中。<lb/>然而，現有的方法通常將指令調整模型視為一個被動的目標，僅在最終合併階段參與，而未能指導訓練過程。<lb/>我們提出了GIFT（Guided Fine-Tuning and Transfer），這是一個簡單而高效的框架，將指令模型的指導融入任務適應中。<lb/>GIFT在預訓練的基礎模型上使用來自指令調整模型的信心信號對低秩適配器進行微調。<lb/>然後，學習到的適配器被合併到指令調整模型中，產生保留一般指令遵循行為的任務專用模型。<lb/>我們在多個模型系列和規模上對數學和知識密集型基準測試進行了GIFT的評估。<lb/>結果顯示，GIFT始終優於直接微調和代表性的基於轉移的基準，同時保持穩健的泛化能力和良好的測試時間擴展行為。
2605.01256.author: Zhiwen Ruan et.al.
2605.01256.authors: Zhiwen Ruan, Yichao Du, Jianjie Zheng, Longyue Wang, Yun Chen, Peng Li, Jinsong Su, Yang Liu, Guanhua Chen
